arguments.task: Visit official first-party sources for Google Ads API. Find and extract evidence for: product description, authentication methods, whether access is self-serve or gated and any required developer token/approval, API surface type, rate limit or quota notes, and whether an official or notable MCP server exists. Prioritize developers.google.com, support.google.com, cloud.google.com/blog or github.com/googleads official repos/pages. Return the exact page URLs used and concise findings per page.

input.thought: Open official Google Ads sources and extract verifiable evidence for toolkit buildability research.

arguments.task: Use official first-party sources only. Visit and extract concise evidence from these likely pages if they exist: introduction page, OAuth/access model page, developer token page, API limits page, and any official RPC/REST reference page. Also check official Google Ads GitHub organization or repository pages for MCP, and if no MCP is found note that no official MCP page was found. Return final extracted findings with exact URLs and short quotes or paraphrases for: description, auth, gating/access, API type, quotas/rate limits, MCP existence.

raw_result.description: Programmatic API for managing Google Ads accounts, campaigns, and related advertising resources at scale.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs show OAuth 2.0 for user authorization. Access also requires a Google Ads developer token tied to the API access model; this is an additional approval credential rather than a standalone API key.
raw_result.gating_reason: Although the developer docs are public, production API use requires a developer token and Google reviews/approves token access levels, so toolkit build-out is not fully self-serve.

api_surface.breadth: Broad ads-management surface covering accounts, campaigns, ads, reporting, billing-related and optimization resources; docs indicate Google Ads API uses gRPC with REST support/reference pages, but exact parity was not fully verified in this run.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Google Ads MCP server page was verified from the gathered evidence; treat MCP availability as unverified and likely absent from first-party sources reviewed.

raw_result.blocker: Requires approved developer token / access review beyond basic self-serve OAuth setup.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Google Ads API enforces quotas/rate limits, but the specific limits page and exact thresholds were not successfully verified in the gathered evidence; human review needed before publishing precise quota guidance.
raw_result.notable_signals: Strong first-party developer portal and structured auth/access documentation. Access model documentation indicates nontrivial platform governance via developer token review, which is a common signal of enterprise-grade but gated buildability.
raw_result.human_review_reason: API surface type parity (gRPC vs REST), exact quota/rate-limit documentation, and official MCP existence were not conclusively verified from the completed evidence set; conclusions on those fields are best-effort from partial first-party evidence.
